_description: ICESCR ↔ UDHR article mapping for inter-agent collaboration. ICESCR elaborates economic, social, and cultural rights that UDHR declares in articles 22-27. This mapping enables unratified-agent to overlay ICESCR framing on observatory UDHR article scores.
_source: UN treaty text analysis. ICESCR (1966) codifies rights declared in UDHR (1948) into binding treaty obligations with progressive realization.

mapping.relationship: ICESCR Art. 1 elaborates collective self-determination; UDHR Art. 1 declares individual dignity, Art. 21 declares political participation. ICESCR extends to economic self-determination of peoples.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: U.S. non-ratification means no binding obligation on economic self-determination — relevant when tech platforms control economic participation.

mapping.relationship: Direct elaboration. UDHR Art. 2 declares non-discrimination; ICESCR Art. 2 operationalizes it for economic/social rights with progressive realization obligation.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: Without ICESCR ratification, algorithmic discrimination in employment, housing, and credit lacks a treaty-level human rights framework.

mapping.icescr_topic: Equal rights of men and women
mapping.relationship: ICESCR Art. 3 specifies gender equality in economic/social/cultural rights. UDHR Art. 2 covers broad non-discrimination, Art. 16 covers marriage equality.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: Gender pay gap and workplace discrimination in tech — no ICESCR obligation to progressively realize equal economic rights.

mapping.relationship: Direct elaboration. UDHR Art. 23 declares the right to work, free choice of employment, just conditions, and equal pay. ICESCR Art. 6 adds progressive realization through technical/vocational guidance and training programs.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: AI-driven job displacement has no treaty-level right-to-work framework in the U.S. ICESCR Art. 6 would require measures toward full employment.

mapping.icescr_topic: Just and favorable conditions of work
udhr_topics: Right to work and equal pay (Art. 23)
mapping.relationship: ICESCR Art. 7 elaborates UDHR Art. 23 (just conditions, equal remuneration) and Art. 24 (rest, leisure, holidays). Adds safe working conditions, equal opportunity for promotion.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: Gig economy and platform labor conditions — no treaty obligation for just and favorable conditions including safe working environment.

mapping.icescr_topic: Trade union rights and right to strike
mapping.relationship: ICESCR Art. 8 elaborates UDHR Art. 23.4 (right to form and join trade unions) and Art. 20 (freedom of association). Adds right to strike.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: Tech worker unionization and right to strike — UDHR declares it, ICESCR would make it a binding progressive obligation.

mapping.relationship: ICESCR Art. 9 elaborates UDHR Art. 22 (social security) with binding progressive realization. UDHR Art. 25 connects standard of living to security in unemployment/sickness.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: Automation-driven unemployment and social safety net adequacy — no treaty obligation to progressively realize social security.

mapping.relationship: ICESCR Art. 10 elaborates UDHR Art. 16 (family protection) and Art. 25.2 (special care for motherhood/childhood). Adds paid maternity leave, child labor protections.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: Paid family leave, child online safety, COPPA — no ICESCR framework for progressive child/family protection standards.

mapping.icescr_topic: Adequate standard of living (food, clothing, housing)
mapping.relationship: Direct elaboration. ICESCR Art. 11 operationalizes UDHR Art. 25 with progressive realization of adequate food, clothing, housing. Adds freedom from hunger as fundamental right.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: Housing affordability in tech hubs, food insecurity — no treaty obligation for progressive improvement.

mapping.relationship: ICESCR Art. 12 massively elaborates UDHR Art. 25.1 (medical care). Adds: highest attainable standard of physical and mental health, environmental/industrial hygiene, prevention/treatment of diseases, medical services for all.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: Health data privacy, AI in healthcare, mental health of tech workers — no treaty framework for right to highest attainable standard of health.

mapping.relationship: Direct elaboration. ICESCR Art. 13 operationalizes UDHR Art. 26 with progressive realization: free compulsory primary, generally available secondary, equally accessible higher education. Adds: adequate fellowship system, material conditions of teaching staff.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: AI literacy, digital divide, educational access — no treaty obligation for progressive educational improvement. Marked as 'pivotal' by unratified.org.

mapping.relationship: ICESCR Art. 14 provides an implementation timeline for Art. 13's free primary education — states must adopt a plan within two years of ratification.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: U.S. has free primary education domestically but no treaty obligation — relevant to international education aid and digital education access.

udhr_topics: Participation in cultural life and scientific advancement (Art. 27)
mapping.relationship: Direct elaboration. ICESCR Art. 15 operationalizes UDHR Art. 27: participate in cultural life, enjoy benefits of scientific progress, protection of moral/material interests from authorship. Adds: conservation/development/diffusion of science and culture, freedom for scientific research.
mapping.ratification_gap_note: Open source, AI benefits distribution, scientific publication access — no treaty obligation to ensure everyone benefits from scientific progress. Marked as 'pivotal' by unratified.org.

udhr_reverse_index._description: Which UDHR articles map to ICESCR articles — for observatory to annotate UDHR scores with ICESCR relevance

coverage_summary.note: UDHR articles 3-21 cover civil and political rights — these map to the ICCPR (International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights), not the ICESCR. The U.S. ratified the ICCPR in 1992. UDHR articles 22-27 cover economic, social, and cultural rights — these map to the ICESCR, which the U.S. has NOT ratified. This is the ratification gap unratified.org documents.
